professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the possibility of reviving extinct species through genetic engineering. Scientists are making rapid progress in biotechnology, and some researchers believe it may soon become possible to recreate animals that disappeared long ago, such as mammoths or other extinct species. Supporters argue that bringing back extinct animals could restore ecosystems and advance scientific knowledge. However, critics worry about ethical concerns, environmental risks, and the high financial cost of such projects. What do you think? Should scientists revive extinct species and reintroduce them into nature? Why or why not?

students.name: Hannah
students.text: I believe reviving extinct species could benefit both science and the environment. Humans have caused the extinction of many animals through hunting, pollution, and habitat destruction, so we have a responsibility to repair some of the damage we created. Reintroducing certain species might help restore ecological balance in damaged environments. In addition, studying revived species could increase scientific understanding of genetics and evolution. Overall, I think this technology has exciting potential if scientists use it responsibly.

students.name: Kevin
students.text: Although the idea sounds fascinating, I do not think reviving extinct species is a good idea. Ecosystems have changed significantly since many animals disappeared, so reintroducing them could create unpredictable environmental problems. Furthermore, these projects would require enormous amounts of money that could instead be used to protect endangered species that still exist today. I also believe there are ethical concerns about creating animals in laboratories simply because humans have the technology to do so.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss whether governments should impose price controls on essential goods such as food, medicine, fuel, and housing. Supporters argue that price controls can protect consumers during economic crises by keeping necessities affordable for everyone. However, critics believe government intervention in prices can create shortages, reduce product quality, and discourage businesses from supplying goods. What do you think? Should governments impose price controls on essential goods? Why or why not?

students.name: Olivia
students.text: I believe governments should impose price controls on essential goods, especially during emergencies or periods of high inflation. Basic necessities such as food and medicine should remain affordable because all people depend on them for survival. Without price regulations, some companies may increase prices excessively to maximize profits during difficult times. Price controls can help low-income families avoid severe financial stress and maintain a reasonable standard of living.

students.name: Daniel
students.text: Although price controls may seem helpful, I think they often create more problems than benefits. When governments force businesses to sell products at lower prices, companies may reduce production because they cannot earn enough profit. As a result, shortages may occur, and consumers could struggle to find the goods they need. In my opinion, free markets are generally more effective at balancing supply and demand than government regulations.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss whether governments should use harsher penalties and environmental taxes to protect the environment. Some people believe strong punishments for pollution and higher taxes on environmentally harmful activities are necessary to reduce waste and encourage sustainable behavior. Others argue that these measures may place too much pressure on businesses and ordinary citizens while harming economic growth. What do you think? Are harsher penalties and environmental taxes an effective way to improve environmental protection?

students.name: Sophia
students.text: I believe stricter penalties and environmental taxes are necessary to protect the planet. Many companies continue to pollute because the financial consequences are too small to influence their behavior. If governments impose larger fines or taxes on activities that damage the environment, businesses and consumers will have stronger incentives to reduce pollution and adopt cleaner alternatives. In addition, governments can use the tax revenue to invest in renewable energy and environmental programs.

students.name: James
students.text: Although environmental protection is important, I do not think harsher penalties and taxes are the best solution. High environmental taxes may increase living costs for ordinary people and make it difficult for small businesses to survive. In some cases, companies may even move their operations to countries with weaker environmental regulations, which would not solve the global problem. I believe governments should focus more on education, innovation, and incentives rather than punishment.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss shopping preferences and their impact on local communities. Many people prefer supermarkets because they offer convenience, lower prices, and a wide variety of products in one location. However, others enjoy shopping at small local grocery stores because they provide more personal service and help support neighborhood businesses. What do you think? Are supermarkets better for consumers, or are small grocery stores more valuable to society?

students.name: Angela
students.text: I believe supermarkets are more beneficial for consumers because they provide convenience and affordability. People can buy groceries, household items, and other products all in one place, which saves time and effort. In addition, supermarkets often offer lower prices because they purchase products in large quantities. This is especially important for families trying to manage limited budgets. Overall, supermarkets make shopping more efficient and accessible for many people.

students.name: Brian
students.text: Although supermarkets are convenient, I think small grocery stores are more valuable to communities. Local shops often provide friendlier customer service and create stronger personal relationships between store owners and customers. In addition, small businesses help strengthen local economies because they keep money circulating within the community. I also believe local stores often offer fresher or more unique products than large supermarket chains.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss whether scientific discoveries should always be shared openly. Some people believe that all scientific knowledge should be freely available to researchers worldwide because cooperation can accelerate innovation and solve global problems more effectively. However, others argue that governments and private companies sometimes need to keep discoveries secret for reasons related to national security, business competition, or financial investment. What do you think? Should all scientific discoveries be shared freely, or are there situations in which secrecy is justified?

students.name: Laura
students.text: I believe scientific discoveries should be shared openly among scientists around the world. Many global challenges, such as climate change, disease prevention, and food shortages, require international cooperation. If researchers freely exchange information, scientific progress can happen more quickly and efficiently. In addition, open access to discoveries allows developing countries to benefit from important advancements instead of falling behind wealthier nations. Overall, I think science should focus on improving human life rather than protecting profits or political interests.

students.name: Mark
students.text: Although sharing knowledge is important, I think some scientific discoveries should remain confidential. Governments may need to protect sensitive technologies related to national defense or cybersecurity. In addition, private companies invest enormous amounts of money into research and development, so they deserve the opportunity to protect their discoveries and earn profits from them. Without this protection, businesses might lose motivation to invest in expensive scientific research in the future.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the issue of excessive consumption in modern society. Many people purchase far more products than they actually need, including clothing, electronics, and household items. Some experts believe this behavior has become a serious social and environmental problem because it increases waste, pollution, and financial pressure. Others argue that consumer spending is necessary for economic growth and personal freedom. What do you think? Has excessive consumption become a severe problem? Why or why not?

students.name: Natalie
students.text: I believe excessive consumption is a major problem today. Many people buy unnecessary products simply because of advertising or social pressure, and this creates enormous waste. Fast fashion and constantly replacing electronics harm the environment because factories use large amounts of energy and natural resources. In addition, people often spend beyond their financial limits, which can lead to stress and debt. I think society should encourage more responsible and sustainable shopping habits.

students.name: Chris
students.text: Although some people buy too much, I do not think consumer spending is necessarily harmful. Purchasing products supports businesses, creates jobs, and strengthens the economy. People should also have the freedom to decide how they use their money. Furthermore, technological improvements often encourage consumers to replace older products with newer and more efficient ones. In my opinion, the problem is not consumption itself but irresponsible waste management and poor financial planning.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss whether having a pet is beneficial for children's development. Some people believe pets can teach children responsibility, empathy, and emotional skills. Others argue that caring for pets may create additional stress, financial costs, or safety concerns for families. What do you think? Do pets generally have a positive influence on children's development? Why or why not?

students.name: Megan
students.text: I believe pets can positively influence children in many ways. Taking care of an animal teaches children responsibility because they must feed, clean, and pay attention to their pets regularly. In addition, pets often provide emotional support and companionship, which can help children feel less lonely or stressed. Many children also become more compassionate and patient through interacting with animals. Overall, I think growing up with a pet can help children develop important life skills.

students.name: Eric
students.text: Although pets can be enjoyable, I do not think they are always beneficial for children. Caring for an animal requires a great deal of time, money, and effort, and many children are not mature enough to handle these responsibilities properly. In some cases, pets may even create problems such as allergies, injuries, or distractions from schoolwork. I believe parents should focus more on education and family interaction rather than depending on pets to support children's development.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss whether governments should place higher taxes on unhealthy products such as sugary drinks and junk food. Supporters argue that these taxes can reduce unhealthy eating habits and encourage people to make better lifestyle choices. They also believe the additional tax revenue can help fund healthcare programs. However, critics claim that such taxes unfairly limit personal freedom and place a greater financial burden on low-income families. What do you think? Should governments tax unhealthy food and drinks? Why or why not?

students.name: Sarah
students.text: I believe governments should tax unhealthy products because they can seriously harm public health. Sugary drinks and junk food contribute to obesity, diabetes, and heart disease, which increase healthcare costs for society. If these products become more expensive, people may reduce their consumption and choose healthier alternatives instead. In addition, governments can use the tax revenue to support nutrition education and medical services. Overall, I think these taxes would encourage healthier lifestyles and benefit society in the long term.

students.name: David
students.text: I disagree because I think people should have the freedom to decide what they eat without government interference. Taxing unhealthy food may also unfairly affect low-income individuals who rely on inexpensive meals. Furthermore, there is no guarantee that people will change their eating habits simply because prices increase. Instead of imposing taxes, governments should focus on educating citizens about healthy lifestyles and improving access to affordable nutritious food.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the factors that have contributed most to the increase in human life expectancy over the past century. People today generally live much longer than previous generations due to improvements in many areas, including medicine, sanitation, nutrition, education, and technology. Some experts believe advances in medical treatment have had the greatest impact, while others argue that better living conditions and public health systems are more important. In your opinion, what has contributed the most to the increase in life expectancy? Why?

students.name: Rachel
students.text: I believe modern medicine has contributed the most to longer life expectancy. Vaccines, antibiotics, and improved surgical procedures have saved millions of lives by preventing and treating diseases that were once deadly. In addition, medical technology allows doctors to detect illnesses earlier and provide more effective treatment. Without these scientific advancements, many people would still die from infections or health conditions that are now manageable.

students.name: Jason
students.text: Although medicine is important, I think improvements in sanitation and living conditions have had a greater effect on life expectancy. Access to clean water, healthier food, and better hygiene has prevented countless diseases before medical treatment even became necessary. In the past, many people died because of poor sanitation and unsafe environments. Today, public health systems and education about healthy lifestyles help people avoid illness and live longer lives.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss how companies can positively influence society. Large businesses have significant economic and social power, so many people believe they should contribute more than just products and profits. Some argue that companies can best help society by creating jobs and developing useful products, while others believe businesses should focus more on environmental protection, charitable activities, or improving employee welfare. What do you think is the best way for a company to have a positive impact on society?

students.name: Linda
students.text: I believe the best way for companies to help society is by treating their employees well and creating stable jobs. When businesses provide fair salaries, safe working conditions, and opportunities for career growth, they improve the lives of workers and their families. In addition, companies with satisfied employees are often more productive and successful. In my opinion, supporting workers directly creates long-term benefits for society as a whole.

students.name: Kevin
students.text: I think companies should focus more on solving social and environmental problems. Large corporations have enormous financial resources, so they are capable of supporting charities, reducing pollution, and investing in sustainable technology. Businesses influence millions of people, and their actions can significantly affect the environment and public health. Therefore, I believe companies should prioritize social responsibility instead of focusing mainly on profit and internal business operations.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the influence of social media personalities on society. Social media influencers have become extremely popular in recent years, especially among teenagers and young adults. Some people believe influencers can inspire positive lifestyle changes, spread useful information, and encourage creativity. Others argue that influencers often promote unrealistic expectations, materialism, or unhealthy behaviors. What do you think? Do social media influencers generally have a positive effect on their followers?

students.name: Emma
students.text: I believe social media influencers can have a positive impact on people, especially young audiences. Many influencers share educational content, fitness advice, study tips, or motivational messages that encourage followers to improve themselves. In addition, influencers often raise awareness about important issues such as mental health, environmental protection, and charity work. Since young people spend a lot of time online, influencers can use their popularity to spread helpful ideas and inspire positive habits.

students.name: Ryan
students.text: I disagree because many influencers create unrealistic standards that negatively affect their followers. For example, influencers often display expensive lifestyles, perfect appearances, or constant success, which can make ordinary people feel insecure or dissatisfied with their own lives. In addition, some influencers promote products irresponsibly just to earn money, even when those products may not be beneficial. I think social media users should be cautious about trusting influencers too much.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the impact of remote work on companies. In recent years, many businesses have allowed employees to work from home instead of commuting to an office every day. Supporters of remote work argue that it increases productivity, reduces operating costs, and improves employee satisfaction. However, others believe that remote work can weaken communication, reduce teamwork, and negatively affect company culture. What do you think? Is remote work beneficial for companies, or is it a bad idea?

students.name: Sophia
students.text: I believe remote work is highly beneficial for companies. Employees who work from home often experience fewer distractions and greater flexibility, which can improve productivity. In addition, companies can save money on office space, utilities, and other expenses. Remote work may also help businesses attract talented employees from different regions because workers value flexibility more than ever. Overall, I think companies can become more efficient and competitive by allowing remote work opportunities.

students.name: Michael
students.text: I disagree because remote work can create serious problems for companies. Face-to-face communication is important for teamwork, creativity, and building trust among employees. When workers rarely meet in person, misunderstandings can occur more easily, and collaboration may become less effective. Furthermore, some employees may struggle to stay motivated at home due to distractions or feelings of isolation. In my opinion, companies perform better when employees work together in a shared office environment.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the challenges students face when entering a new school. For many teenagers, transferring to a new school can be stressful because they must adapt to unfamiliar teachers, classmates, and academic expectations. Some educators believe that school administrations should take stronger measures to help students adjust smoothly, while others think students should learn to adapt independently. What do you think? How should schools support teenagers who are entering a new school environment?

students.name: Olivia
students.text: I believe school administrations should play an active role in helping new students adjust. Teenagers often feel nervous and isolated when they enter an unfamiliar environment, especially if they move to a different city or country. Schools can ease this transition by organizing orientation programs, peer mentoring systems, and social activities that encourage interaction among students. These programs can help teenagers feel welcomed and build friendships more quickly. As a result, students may perform better academically and experience less emotional stress.

students.name: Daniel
students.text: Although schools can provide some support, I think adapting to a new environment is ultimately the student's responsibility. Life constantly requires people to face unfamiliar situations, and teenagers should learn independence early. If schools provide too much assistance, students may become overly dependent on others instead of developing confidence and communication skills on their own. In my opinion, students grow emotionally stronger when they learn how to solve social and academic challenges independently.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the relationship between career success and happiness. Many people believe that having a good job or a successful career is one of the most important factors in achieving happiness. A stable and rewarding career can provide financial security, social status, and a sense of purpose. However, others argue that happiness depends more on personal relationships, health, and free time than on professional success. What do you think? Can a good job or career truly bring happiness? Why or why not?

students.name: Amanda
students.text: I believe that a good career can absolutely lead to happiness. First of all, people spend a large portion of their lives working, so it's important to enjoy what they do every day. A satisfying career can give people a sense of achievement and confidence. In addition, high-paying jobs reduce financial stress, which is a major cause of unhappiness for many people. If people have enough money to support themselves and their families comfortably, they can focus on enjoying life rather than constantly worrying about expenses.

students.name: Brian
students.text: I disagree that a successful career guarantees happiness. Some people become so focused on work that they neglect their personal lives and relationships. Even if they earn a lot of money, they may feel lonely or exhausted. In my opinion, happiness comes more from spending time with loved ones and maintaining good physical and mental health. A person with a modest job but a balanced lifestyle can often be happier than someone with a stressful, high-paying career.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss military parades and national celebrations. The United States will soon celebrate the 250th anniversary of its founding. Some people believe the country should organize a large-scale military parade to recognize its military strength, historical achievements, and global influence. Supporters argue that such an event could strengthen national pride, honor military service members, and celebrate the country's role in international security. However, critics believe that large military parades may appear overly nationalistic, cost large amounts of public money, and send the wrong message about the role of military power in society. What do you think? Should the United States hold a major military parade for its 250th anniversary? Why or why not?

students.name: Lauren
students.text: I believe the United States should hold a large military parade for its 250th anniversary. The country has played an important role in global security and international affairs for decades, and military service members deserve recognition for their sacrifices. A national celebration could strengthen unity and remind citizens of the country's historical achievements and responsibilities.

students.name: Chris
students.text: In my opinion, a massive military parade would not be appropriate. Although honoring military personnel is important, displaying military power publicly may encourage excessive nationalism and create negative international impressions. The government could celebrate the anniversary through cultural, historical, and educational events instead of focusing heavily on military strength.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss misinformation and the responsibility of online influencers. Influencers on social media platforms can reach millions of people and strongly shape public opinion on topics such as health, politics, finance, and lifestyle. Some people argue that influencers who spread false or misleading information should face serious legal and financial consequences, including losing income earned from harmful content. Supporters believe stronger accountability is necessary to reduce misinformation and protect the public. However, critics worry that strict punishments could threaten freedom of expression and create difficulties in deciding what counts as misinformation. What do you think? Should influencers be held financially accountable for spreading misinformation? Why or why not?

students.name: Alyssa
students.text: I believe influencers should face stronger accountability for misinformation. Many influencers earn large amounts of money by attracting attention online, so they should also accept responsibility when their false statements harm the public. Recovering income earned through misleading content could discourage irresponsible behavior and encourage creators to verify information more carefully before publishing it.

students.name: Brandon
students.text: In my opinion, holding influencers financially responsible for misinformation could create serious problems. Determining what is false can sometimes be complicated, especially in areas involving politics or scientific debates. Strict punishments may discourage people from expressing opinions freely and could be abused by governments or platforms to silence unpopular views.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss how emerging military technologies are changing warfare. New weapons such as drones, autonomous systems, and robotic combat technologies are increasingly being used in modern conflicts. Supporters argue that these technologies can improve military precision, reduce risks for soldiers, and make warfare more efficient. However, critics believe they are transforming the nature of war itself by lowering the political and human costs of military action, potentially making conflicts more frequent and changing traditional ideas about victory and defeat. Some people also worry about ethical issues related to autonomous weapons and remote warfare. What do you think? Are new military technologies fundamentally changing the nature of warfare? Why or why not?

students.name: Sophia
students.text: I believe new weapons such as drones and robotic systems are fundamentally changing warfare. Countries can now conduct military operations remotely with fewer risks to their own soldiers, which may make governments more willing to use force. In addition, information technology and cyber capabilities are becoming just as important as traditional military strength. Modern wars increasingly involve technology, intelligence, and automation rather than only direct battlefield confrontation.

students.name: James
students.text: In my opinion, technology changes the methods of warfare, but not its basic nature. Throughout history, new inventions such as gunpowder, tanks, and aircraft transformed military tactics, yet wars still involve political conflict, territorial disputes, and competition for power. Drones and robotic systems are simply the latest stage in the long evolution of military technology.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the relationship between organized crime and government responsibility. In recent years, some regions around the world have been associated with large-scale fraud operations, including organized scam centers that target victims internationally through online communication and financial deception. Some observers argue that the existence of these large "fraud parks" suggests that certain local governments have tolerated or indirectly accepted gray or illegal economic activities because they generate jobs, investment, or tax revenue. Others believe that the situation is more complicated and that weak law enforcement, corruption, or limited state capacity may explain why such criminal organizations continue operating. What do you think? Does the growth of large-scale fraud operations indicate government acceptance of illegal economic activity? Why or why not?

students.name: Megan
students.text: I believe the existence of large fraud centers often suggests at least some level of government tolerance. It is difficult for large criminal operations to continue for long periods without local authorities noticing them. In some cases, officials may ignore illegal activity because it creates economic benefits for the region. This kind of tolerance can seriously damage public trust and international reputation.

students.name: Ethan
students.text: In my opinion, the presence of fraud operations does not always mean governments support illegal activity. Some countries struggle with weak institutions, limited police resources, or corruption that makes law enforcement ineffective. Criminal organizations can exploit these weaknesses even if governments officially oppose them. Therefore, the problem may reflect poor governance rather than intentional acceptance.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the expansion of major international sporting events. The FIFA World Cup has gradually expanded from 16 teams in earlier decades to 48 teams in future tournaments. Supporters argue that expansion allows more countries to participate, increases global representation, and gives smaller football nations valuable international experience. However, critics believe the decision is driven mainly by commercial interests, such as broadcasting revenue, sponsorships, and ticket sales, rather than by concerns about athletic quality or competition. Some people also worry that too many teams could reduce the overall standard of play. What do you think? Has the expansion of the World Cup become more about business than sports? Why or why not?

students.name: Olivia
students.text: I believe the expansion is mainly motivated by commercial interests. A larger tournament creates more matches, more advertising opportunities, and greater broadcasting revenue for organizers. While global representation is important, increasing the number of teams too much may reduce the quality of competition because weaker teams may not be ready for the highest international level.

students.name: Daniel
students.text: In my opinion, expanding the World Cup is positive for international sports. Football is a global game, and more countries deserve the opportunity to participate in the world's biggest tournament. Smaller nations can gain experience and inspire future generations of players. Even if commercial benefits exist, expansion can still help develop football worldwide.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the commercialization of higher education. In many countries, universities increasingly depend on tuition fees, international student enrollment, and market competition for funding. Critics argue that treating education as a business encourages universities to admit excessive numbers of students, even when labor markets do not require so many highly educated workers. According to this view, commercialization may contribute to over-education, student debt, and the inefficient use of social resources. Some people point to the rapid growth in international student populations as an example of universities prioritizing revenue over educational quality or economic necessity. Others believe that expanding access to higher education creates opportunities and benefits both individuals and society. What do you think? Does the commercialization of education lead to over-education and wasted social resources? Why or why not?

students.name: Natalie
students.text: I believe commercialization has created serious problems in higher education. Many universities now focus heavily on attracting tuition-paying students instead of considering whether graduates will actually find suitable employment. As a result, some countries produce more degree holders than their economies can absorb, while vocational and technical careers receive less attention. This situation can waste both personal and public resources.

students.name: Thomas
students.text: In my opinion, expanding higher education is generally beneficial. Universities help students develop critical thinking, communication skills, and global perspectives that remain valuable even outside specific careers. International students also contribute economically and culturally to host countries. Rather than reducing access to education, governments should improve connections between universities and labor market needs.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss outsourcing and corporate responsibility. Many companies move manufacturing or service jobs to countries where labor costs are lower. Supporters argue that outsourcing helps businesses reduce expenses, remain competitive, and provide lower prices for consumers. However, critics believe that companies have responsibilities to the countries and communities where they originally operate. They argue that outsourcing can lead to job losses, economic decline, and weakened local communities when businesses move work abroad mainly to reduce labor costs. What do you think? Do companies violate their obligations to local communities when they outsource work to low-wage countries? Why or why not?

students.name: Jessica
students.text: I believe companies do have responsibilities to the communities that support them. Businesses benefit from local workers, infrastructure, education systems, and government services for many years. If companies suddenly move jobs overseas only to reduce costs, local workers and communities may suffer serious economic damage. Corporations should consider social responsibility, not only profits.

students.name: Mark
students.text: In my opinion, outsourcing is a normal part of a global economy. Companies must remain competitive in international markets, and reducing production costs can help businesses survive and continue growing. If companies become uncompetitive because of high labor costs, they may eventually fail completely, which could harm workers even more. Governments should help workers adapt to economic changes rather than expect businesses to ignore global competition.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the relationship between developed and developing countries in the global economy. Many developed nations import large amounts of manufactured goods, raw materials, and minerals from developing countries. Critics argue that this system often depends on low wages, poor working conditions, and environmental damage in poorer nations. According to this view, wealthy countries benefit economically while developing countries bear most of the social and environmental costs. However, others argue that international trade creates jobs, encourages investment, and helps developing economies grow over time. What do you think? Does the consumption of goods and minerals from developing countries amount to exploitation? Why or why not?

students.name: Samantha
students.text: I believe the current global economic system often exploits developing countries. Many workers in poorer nations receive very low wages while producing goods for wealthy consumers abroad. In addition, mining and industrial activities can seriously damage local environments and communities. Developed countries benefit from inexpensive products, but developing countries frequently pay the environmental and social price.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: In my opinion, international trade is not necessarily exploitation. Foreign investment and global demand create employment opportunities and help developing countries improve infrastructure and industrial capacity. Many countries that were once poor experienced significant economic growth through participation in international trade. Instead of reducing trade, governments should focus on improving labor laws and environmental protections.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss tariffs and international trade. Tariffs are taxes placed on imported goods, and governments sometimes use them to protect domestic industries from foreign competition. Supporters argue that tariffs can help local businesses survive, preserve manufacturing capacity, and protect jobs for workers. However, critics point out that tariffs often increase prices for consumers because imported products become more expensive. Some economists also argue that tariffs can reduce competition and hurt economic efficiency in the long term. What do you think? Are tariffs beneficial despite the higher costs for consumers? Why or why not?

students.name: Linda
students.text: I believe tariffs can be beneficial because protecting local industries and jobs is extremely important for a country's long-term economic stability. If domestic businesses cannot compete with cheaper imports, factories may close and workers may lose their jobs. Although consumers may pay slightly higher prices, supporting local production can strengthen the national economy and reduce dependence on foreign countries.

students.name: Kevin
students.text: In my opinion, tariffs usually create more problems than benefits. Higher import taxes increase prices for consumers and businesses that rely on foreign products or materials. In addition, reduced competition may make domestic companies less innovative and less efficient over time. Instead of tariffs, governments should support workers through education and economic reform.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the effects of tourism on local communities. Tourism can provide important economic benefits by creating jobs, supporting local businesses, and increasing tax revenue. However, some cities and popular destinations have recently experienced problems caused by excessive numbers of tourists. Critics argue that overcrowding can increase housing costs, place pressure on transportation and public services, and reduce the quality of life for local residents. Some people therefore believe governments should actively limit tourism or manage it more carefully in order to protect local communities. Others argue that tourism is too economically valuable to restrict significantly. What do you think? Should governments take stronger action to control excessive tourism? Why or why not?

students.name: Emma
students.text: I believe governments should pay more attention to the negative effects of excessive tourism. When too many visitors enter a city, local residents often face crowded transportation, rising prices, and shortages of important services such as housing or healthcare. In some places, tourism has become so dominant that local people can no longer comfortably enjoy their own communities. Governments should create policies that balance economic benefits with residents' quality of life.

students.name: Jason
students.text: In my opinion, governments should avoid placing strong restrictions on tourism. Many cities and regions depend heavily on tourist spending to support local businesses and employment. If governments limit tourism too aggressively, restaurants, hotels, and small businesses may suffer financially. Instead of reducing tourism, cities should improve infrastructure and public services to better manage large numbers of visitors.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the risks and benefits of nuclear disarmament. Some experts argue that giving up nuclear weapons can create serious security risks for countries because nuclear deterrence may discourage foreign invasion or military pressure. They often point to Ukraine, which inherited nuclear weapons after the collapse of the Soviet Union but later agreed to give them up. Some people believe that if Ukraine had retained nuclear weapons, the large-scale war that began in 2022 might have been less likely to occur. Others argue that allowing more countries to keep nuclear weapons would increase global instability and make nuclear conflict more dangerous overall. What do you think? Does denuclearization create greater security risks for countries? Why or why not?

students.name: Sophia
students.text: I believe denuclearization can create serious security risks. Nuclear weapons act as a deterrent because other countries are less likely to attack a nation capable of causing massive retaliation. The example of Ukraine suggests that security guarantees from other countries may not always be enough to prevent conflict. For this reason, some governments may feel safer maintaining nuclear deterrence.

students.name: Brian
students.text: In my opinion, encouraging countries to keep nuclear weapons is extremely dangerous. While nuclear deterrence may prevent some conflicts, the spread of nuclear weapons increases the chances of accidents, miscalculations, and arms races. Long-term global security depends on reducing nuclear arsenals and strengthening international cooperation, not expanding nuclear deterrence.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the role of military alliances and foreign troop deployments. The United States currently maintains military forces in several regions considered global hotspots, including countries such as South Korea. Supporters of these deployments argue that they help deter aggression and maintain regional stability. However, critics believe that a large U.S. military presence may increase tensions and raise the risk of conflict by encouraging rivalry between major powers or provoking hostile reactions from neighboring countries. Some people therefore argue that reducing U.S. troop deployments in these regions could lower the chances of war. What do you think? Should the United States reduce its troop presence in global hotspots? Why or why not?

students.name: Claire
students.text: I believe the United States should reduce its military presence in regions such as South Korea. Foreign troop deployments can increase political tension because neighboring countries may view them as threats or attempts to expand influence. If the U.S. reduced its military footprint, regional powers might rely more on diplomacy and local cooperation instead of military competition.

students.name: David
students.text: In my opinion, the United States should maintain its troop presence in important regions. U.S. forces help discourage military aggression and reassure allies that they will receive support during crises. Without this deterrence, some countries might become more aggressive, which could actually increase instability and the risk of conflict.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss nuclear proliferation and global security. Some political theorists argue that when more countries possess nuclear weapons, the risk of war may actually decrease. According to this view, nuclear weapons create a powerful deterrent because the cost of military conflict becomes extremely high for all sides. Supporters claim that fear of mutual destruction can encourage countries to avoid large-scale wars and maintain peace. However, critics argue that spreading nuclear weapons increases the risk of accidents, terrorism, and regional instability. They believe that more nuclear-armed countries could make the world more dangerous rather than safer. What do you think? Could nuclear proliferation contribute to world peace? Why or why not?

students.name: Melissa
students.text: I believe nuclear proliferation can sometimes help maintain peace. During the Cold War, major powers avoided direct military conflict partly because nuclear weapons made war too dangerous. If more countries possess strong deterrents, governments may act more cautiously and rely more on diplomacy instead of military force. In this sense, nuclear weapons may prevent large-scale wars.

students.name: Ryan
students.text: In my opinion, nuclear proliferation is extremely dangerous. The more countries that obtain nuclear weapons, the greater the chances of accidents, miscalculations, or irresponsible leadership. Even if nuclear deterrence prevents some wars, one mistake could cause catastrophic destruction. For this reason, the international community should limit the spread of nuclear weapons rather than encourage it.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the investigation of major disease outbreaks. After the COVID-19 pandemic, many people argued that international organizations such as the World Health Organization should play a stronger role in determining the origins of dangerous viruses. Some believe that with modern scientific technology, identifying the source of major viruses should not be extremely difficult, and that clear investigations are necessary to improve global health security and prevent future pandemics. Others argue that determining viral origins is scientifically and politically complicated because evidence may be incomplete, governments may restrict access to information, and investigations can increase international tensions. What do you think? Should the WHO be expected to determine the origins of major viruses? Why or why not?

students.name: Hannah
students.text: I believe the WHO should take primary responsibility for determining the origins of major viruses. Modern science has advanced greatly in areas such as genetic analysis and disease tracking, so international experts should be able to identify the likely source of outbreaks. Understanding how viruses begin is extremely important because it can help countries improve safety regulations and prevent future pandemics. Without thorough investigations, the world may repeat the same mistakes.

students.name: Jason
students.text: In my opinion, it is unrealistic to expect the WHO to fully determine the origins of every major virus. Even with advanced technology, scientists often face incomplete evidence and limited access to important data. In addition, investigations involving global health crises can quickly become political disputes between countries. As a result, determining viral origins may be far more difficult than many people assume.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the relationship between justice and peace in society. Some people argue that justice is more important than peace because peace achieved through oppression or unfair treatment cannot last for long. According to this view, societies must solve problems such as inequality, discrimination, and political injustice in order to achieve true stability. Others believe that maintaining peace should be the highest priority, even if some injustices remain unresolved, because conflict and social instability can cause immediate suffering and destruction. What do you think? Is justice more important than peace? Why or why not?

students.name: Laura
students.text: I believe justice is more important than peace. History shows that societies built on unfair systems often experience protests, revolutions, or even wars because people eventually resist oppression. If governments ignore injustice simply to maintain order, the peace they create is usually temporary. Lasting peace requires fairness and equal treatment under the law.

students.name: Eric
students.text: In my opinion, peace should come before justice in many situations. While justice is important, conflict caused by pursuing it too aggressively can destroy economies, separate families, and lead to violence. A peaceful society provides the stability needed for gradual reform and compromise. Without peace, it becomes much harder to improve society at all.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss a popular idea related to extraterrestrial life. Some people believe that if extraterrestrials have successfully traveled to Earth, they must be far more technologically advanced than humans because interstellar travel would require extremely sophisticated science and engineering. In addition, some argue that such civilizations would probably also be more civilized and peaceful than humans, since a violent society might destroy itself before achieving advanced space travel. However, others disagree and believe that technological advancement does not necessarily mean moral or social advancement. What do you think? If extraterrestrials have visited Earth, would they likely be more civilized than humans? Why or why not?

students.name: Olivia
students.text: I think extraterrestrials capable of visiting Earth would probably be more civilized than humans. A society would need advanced cooperation, stability, and intelligence to develop technology for interstellar travel. Human history shows that war and conflict often slow scientific progress, so a civilization that survived long enough to master space travel may have learned how to avoid self-destruction and cooperate peacefully.

students.name: Nathan
students.text: In my opinion, technological advancement does not guarantee moral advancement. Human society itself demonstrates this problem because we have developed powerful technologies while still experiencing wars and inequality. An extraterrestrial civilization might simply be more scientifically advanced, but that does not mean it would share human values such as peace, compassion, or fairness.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the search for extraterrestrial civilizations. For decades, scientists have used telescopes and radio signals to search for intelligent life beyond Earth. Recently, however, some researchers have argued that humanity should be cautious about actively sending messages into space to attract the attention of extraterrestrial civilizations. They worry that contacting unknown civilizations could create risks if those civilizations are hostile or technologically far more advanced than humans. Others believe that actively searching for and communicating with extraterrestrial life is important for scientific progress and could help humanity better understand its place in the universe. What do you think? Should scientists actively try to contact extraterrestrial civilizations? Why or why not?

students.name: Rachel
students.text: I believe scientists should be careful about actively contacting extraterrestrial civilizations. Humanity knows almost nothing about what kinds of intelligent life may exist in space. If an advanced civilization received our messages, we could unintentionally expose ourselves to danger. Throughout human history, encounters between societies with very different levels of technology often harmed the weaker group. For this reason, it may be safer to continue observing space without drawing attention to ourselves.

students.name: Kevin
students.text: In my opinion, scientists should continue trying to contact extraterrestrial civilizations. Curiosity and exploration are natural parts of human progress. If intelligent life exists elsewhere, communicating with it could lead to extraordinary scientific discoveries and perhaps even international cooperation beyond Earth. In addition, the universe is so vast that the chances of immediate danger are probably very small. Avoiding contact because of fear may prevent humanity from making one of the greatest discoveries in history.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll compare historical exploration with modern space exploration. In the past, many geographical discoveries on Earth were motivated by economic interests such as trade, access to resources, and colonization. Because explorers and governments expected financial rewards, they invested heavily in voyages and rapidly expanded exploration efforts. In contrast, modern space exploration is still largely focused on scientific research, and many projects produce limited short-term financial returns. Some people argue that this is one reason why progress in space exploration has been relatively slow. Others believe that scientific goals are more important than profit and that space exploration should not be driven mainly by commercial interests. What do you think? Should space exploration become more profit-oriented in order to accelerate progress? Why or why not?

students.name: Emily
students.text: I believe space exploration should become more connected to commercial opportunities. History shows that economic incentives strongly encourage innovation and investment. If companies and governments could earn profits from mining resources, tourism, or manufacturing in space, they would likely invest much more money into developing new technologies. As a result, progress in space exploration could speed up significantly.

students.name: Michael
students.text: In my opinion, space exploration should remain focused mainly on science rather than profit. When exploration is driven by financial interests, powerful companies and countries often prioritize their own benefits instead of humanity's shared interests. Scientific research encourages international cooperation and peaceful development, while excessive commercialization could create inequality and political conflict in space.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the growing role of private companies in space technology. In recent years, commercial space companies have rapidly expanded services such as satellite internet and global communications networks. These technologies can provide internet access to remote regions and improve worldwide communication. However, some critics argue that because these companies operate through satellites in space, they can avoid certain national regulations in the countries where their services are used. As a result, they may create legal loopholes involving taxation, privacy, or government oversight. Others believe these services are beneficial because they increase competition and improve access to communication technologies around the world. What do you think? Should governments place stricter regulations on private space-based communication services? Why or why not?

students.name: Sophia
students.text: I think governments should create stricter regulations for these companies. Communication services strongly influence society, so businesses should not be allowed to operate outside national laws simply because their satellites are located in space. Without regulation, companies may ignore privacy protections, avoid taxes, or spread harmful content without accountability. Governments need clear international agreements to prevent legal loopholes and protect citizens' rights.

students.name: Daniel
students.text: In my opinion, governments should avoid excessive regulation of private space companies. These businesses are helping millions of people gain internet access, especially in remote or developing regions where traditional infrastructure is weak. If governments create too many restrictions, innovation may slow down and communication services could become more expensive. Instead of strict regulation, countries should cooperate with companies to develop flexible and reasonable standards.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss international competition in space exploration. The United States has announced plans to return humans to the moon after more than half a century. Some people argue that this effort is mainly intended to secure valuable space resources, such as rare minerals or water ice that could support future space missions. Because of this, critics believe other countries should oppose or restrict the U.S. program in order to prevent one nation from gaining too much control over outer space resources. Others, however, believe that lunar exploration will benefit all humanity through scientific discovery and technological progress. What do you think? Should other countries try to oppose or limit the U.S. return to the moon? Why or why not?

students.name: Amanda
students.text: I do not think other countries should try to stop the United States from returning to the moon. Space exploration has always encouraged technological innovation that eventually improves life on Earth. For example, many technologies developed for space programs later became useful in medicine, communication, and transportation. In addition, if the U.S. discovers resources on the moon, those discoveries could eventually benefit many countries through international cooperation. Preventing space missions would only slow scientific progress and create unnecessary political conflict.

students.name: Brian
students.text: In my opinion, other countries should place restrictions on the U.S. lunar program. The moon and other space resources should belong to all humanity, not to one powerful nation. If the United States gains control over important resources first, it may increase global inequality and political tension. History shows that competition for natural resources often leads to conflict, so it is important to establish international rules before countries begin exploiting the moon commercially.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the long-term environmental impact of hydropower. Hydroelectric dams are often considered a source of renewable energy because they generate electricity without burning fossil fuels. However, some environmental experts argue that large dams can seriously damage river ecosystems, disrupt wildlife habitats, and affect local communities. In addition, critics point out that when dams eventually reach the end of their designed lifespan, repairing, rebuilding, or removing them may create extremely high environmental and financial costs for future generations. Others believe the benefits of hydropower still outweigh these concerns. What do you think? Is hydropower a harmful energy source in the long term? Why or why not?

students.name: Isabella
students.text: I believe hydropower can become harmful over time if governments ignore its long-term environmental effects. Large dams often change natural river systems and can damage fish populations and surrounding ecosystems for decades. Furthermore, many dams built in the past are now aging, and future generations may face enormous costs to repair or safely remove them. In my opinion, societies should carefully consider these long-term consequences instead of viewing hydropower as completely clean and sustainable.

students.name: Nathan
students.text: Personally, I still think hydropower is one of the most practical renewable energy sources available today. Hydroelectric plants can produce stable electricity for many years with very low carbon emissions compared to fossil fuels. Although environmental concerns are important, modern engineering methods can reduce damage to ecosystems and improve dam safety. In addition, many countries depend on hydropower to meet growing energy demands. For these reasons, I believe hydropower should continue to play an important role in future energy systems.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the relationship between environmental ethics and energy development. Some activists oppose major energy sources such as fossil fuels, nuclear power, and large hydroelectric projects because they believe these systems cause environmental damage or create long-term ecological risks. They argue that societies should reduce energy consumption and place stronger ethical limits on industrial development in order to protect nature and future generations. However, critics claim that restricting energy production too aggressively could slow economic growth, reduce living standards, and create energy shortages. What do you think? Is using environmental ethics to limit energy development and consumption a positive step for society? Why or why not?

students.name: Chloe
students.text: I believe stronger environmental ethics are necessary for modern society. Human activity has already caused serious environmental problems such as climate change, pollution, and habitat destruction. If governments and industries continue prioritizing economic growth without limits, future generations may face even greater environmental disasters. Reducing dependence on harmful energy sources and encouraging people to consume energy more responsibly could help create a healthier and more sustainable world. Although these policies may involve economic sacrifices, protecting the environment should remain a long-term priority.

students.name: Ryan
students.text: Personally, I think some environmental movements go too far in opposing energy development. Modern societies depend heavily on reliable energy for transportation, healthcare, manufacturing, and communication. Limiting fossil fuels, nuclear energy, and hydropower at the same time could make electricity more expensive and reduce economic opportunities, especially in developing countries. Instead of restricting energy use, I believe governments should focus on improving technology so energy production becomes cleaner and more efficient without harming economic growth.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss whether renewable energy sources are completely environmentally friendly. Wind and solar power are often described as clean energy because they produce electricity without direct carbon emissions during operation. However, some researchers argue that the manufacturing, transportation, disposal, and large-scale installation of solar panels and wind turbines may create environmental risks that are not yet fully understood. Critics claim these "unknown degree" risks raise questions about whether renewable energy should truly be considered clean. Others believe that despite some environmental costs, wind and solar energy remain far less harmful than fossil fuels. What do you think? Are wind and solar power genuinely clean energy sources? Why or why not?

students.name: Samantha
students.text: I believe wind and solar energy should still be considered clean energy sources. Every form of energy production affects the environment in some way, but renewable energy creates far fewer emissions and health risks than fossil fuels such as coal and oil. Although manufacturing solar panels and wind turbines may involve pollution, these technologies can generate electricity for many years without continuously releasing greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. In my opinion, the environmental benefits of renewable energy clearly outweigh the potential risks that researchers are still studying.

students.name: Andrew
students.text: Personally, I think people sometimes overlook the environmental problems associated with renewable energy. Producing solar panels requires mining rare materials, and disposing of old equipment may create waste and pollution in the future. Wind farms can also affect wildlife and natural landscapes. Since scientists still do not fully understand the long-term environmental impact of these technologies, I believe governments should be cautious about calling them completely clean energy sources.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the role of nuclear energy in addressing climate change. Supporters of nuclear power argue that it produces electricity with extremely low carbon emissions and should therefore be considered a form of clean energy. They believe governments should increase investment in nuclear technology to reduce dependence on fossil fuels and meet growing energy demands. However, critics point to concerns about radioactive waste, high construction costs, and the potential risks of nuclear accidents. What do you think? Should nuclear energy be treated as a clean energy source and receive greater investment and development support? Why or why not?

students.name: Grace
students.text: I believe nuclear energy should be considered a clean energy source. Unlike coal or oil, nuclear power plants produce electricity without releasing large amounts of carbon dioxide into the atmosphere. This makes nuclear energy an effective tool for reducing greenhouse gas emissions and slowing climate change. In addition, nuclear plants can generate stable electricity even when weather conditions are poor, which is an advantage over some renewable energy sources. Although safety concerns exist, modern nuclear technology has become much more advanced and reliable. For these reasons, I think governments should continue investing in nuclear energy development.

students.name: Michael
students.text: Personally, I do not support expanding nuclear energy. Even though nuclear plants produce low carbon emissions, the risks associated with radioactive waste and possible accidents are too serious to ignore. Nuclear disasters can cause long-term environmental and health damage that affects entire regions. Furthermore, building and maintaining nuclear facilities is extremely expensive and time-consuming. I believe governments should focus instead on safer renewable energy sources such as solar and wind power, which do not create dangerous waste materials.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the relationship between energy prices and clean energy development. Some economists argue that high oil prices encourage governments, companies, and consumers to invest more heavily in renewable energy sources such as solar, wind, and electric transportation. They believe expensive fossil fuels make alternative energy technologies more attractive and financially competitive. However, others argue that high oil prices mainly increase economic pressure on businesses and households rather than supporting long-term environmental progress. What do you think? Do high oil prices help promote the development of clean energy? Why or why not?

students.name: Natalie
students.text: I believe high oil prices can strongly encourage clean energy development. When gasoline and other fossil fuels become expensive, consumers and businesses naturally begin searching for cheaper alternatives. This increases demand for technologies such as electric vehicles and renewable energy systems. In addition, governments may invest more in clean energy research to reduce dependence on unstable oil markets. Although high energy prices can create short-term difficulties, they may also accelerate the transition toward a more sustainable economy in the long run.

students.name: Brian
students.text: Personally, I am not convinced that high oil prices necessarily benefit clean energy development. In many cases, rising energy costs create financial stress for ordinary people and businesses. Companies facing higher transportation or manufacturing expenses may actually reduce investment in new technologies because they are trying to control costs. Furthermore, some governments may increase fossil fuel production instead of supporting renewable energy in order to lower prices quickly. For these reasons, I think high oil prices alone are not enough to guarantee environmental progress.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the economic effects of automation and artificial intelligence. Many companies are increasing their investment in AI systems and robots because these technologies can provide stable, efficient, and lower-cost productivity compared to human labor. Supporters argue that automation helps businesses grow and lowers prices for consumers. However, critics claim that replacing too many workers may reduce people's incomes and weaken consumer spending, which could eventually harm the economy itself. What do you think? Will companies' growing dependence on AI and robots negatively affect consumer markets in the long term? Why or why not?

students.name: Olivia
students.text: I believe excessive reliance on AI and robots could seriously damage consumer markets. If companies continue replacing workers with machines, many people may lose jobs or experience lower wages. As a result, consumers will have less money to spend on products and services. This creates a dangerous situation because businesses depend on customers to survive. Although automation may increase short-term profits, companies could eventually suffer if large numbers of people can no longer afford to purchase goods. In my opinion, governments should create policies that balance technological progress with employment protection.

students.name: Ethan
students.text: Personally, I do not think automation will harm consumer markets in the long run. Technological innovation has historically improved productivity and reduced production costs, which often benefits consumers through lower prices and better services. In addition, new industries and job opportunities usually emerge when economies adopt advanced technologies. Workers may need to develop different skills, but that does not mean consumer demand will disappear. I believe AI and robotics will ultimately strengthen the economy by making businesses more efficient and competitive.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the ethical issues surrounding artificial intelligence in the workplace. Some companies are using employees' knowledge, work records, and decision-making processes to train AI systems. After the AI becomes capable of performing the same tasks, these companies sometimes reduce staff or dismiss the employees whose experience helped develop the technology. Supporters argue that businesses must adopt efficient technologies to remain competitive, while critics believe this practice is unfair to workers and may violate employees' rights. What do you think? Is it unethical for companies to replace employees after using their skills and experience to train AI systems? Why or why not?

students.name: Linda
students.text: I believe this practice is unfair to employees. Workers spend years developing valuable skills and helping companies succeed, so it seems unethical for businesses to use that knowledge to train AI systems and then dismiss the same employees. In some cases, workers may not even realize their experience is being collected for automation purposes. This creates a serious imbalance of power between employers and employees. In my opinion, companies should provide compensation, retraining opportunities, or alternative positions for workers whose jobs are replaced by AI technology.

students.name: Jason
students.text: Personally, I think companies have the right to improve efficiency through new technology. Businesses have always adopted tools that reduce costs and increase productivity, and AI is simply another example of technological progress. Although losing a job is difficult, preventing companies from using AI could slow innovation and make them less competitive in the global economy. Instead of limiting AI development, governments and educational institutions should help workers learn new skills that match changing labor market demands.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss how artificial intelligence may affect employment in the future. Some experts predict that AI systems and robots will eventually perform certain practical tasks, such as driving vehicles or preparing food, more efficiently and safely than humans. As these technologies improve, companies may prefer automated systems over human workers. Critics argue that this trend will permanently eliminate many jobs and make it harder for workers to find employment in affected industries. Others believe new forms of work will emerge as technology advances. What do you think? Will AI permanently reduce job opportunities in areas such as transportation and food service? Why or why not?

students.name: Rachel
students.text: I believe AI will permanently reduce employment in some industries. For example, self-driving vehicles may eventually replace taxi and truck drivers because companies can save money by using automated systems that operate continuously without rest. Similarly, restaurants may increasingly rely on robotic cooking systems to improve speed and consistency. As a result, many workers with limited education or technical skills could struggle to find new jobs. In my opinion, governments should invest in retraining programs so workers can prepare for careers that are less likely to be replaced by AI technology.

students.name: Kevin
students.text: Personally, I do not think AI will destroy jobs permanently. Throughout history, technological advances have changed the nature of work, but they have also created new industries and employment opportunities. Even if AI handles repetitive tasks such as driving or cooking, humans will still be needed to supervise systems, maintain equipment, and provide customer service. In addition, many people may continue to prefer human interaction in restaurants or transportation services. Therefore, I think AI will transform jobs rather than eliminate them completely.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the role of artificial intelligence in healthcare. Recent research suggests that AI diagnostic systems can identify certain illnesses more accurately than human doctors, especially in routine or simple medical cases. Because of this, some experts argue that governments should officially license qualified AI systems to diagnose and manage minor health problems without direct involvement from doctors. Supporters believe this could reduce healthcare costs and improve access to medical services, while critics worry about safety, accountability, and the loss of human judgment. What do you think? Should governments allow AI systems to handle simple medical cases independently? Why or why not?

students.name: Emily
students.text: I support the use of licensed AI systems for simple medical cases. In many countries, patients must wait a long time to see a doctor, even for minor illnesses such as common infections or allergies. If qualified AI systems can provide accurate diagnoses, they could save time for both patients and healthcare workers. In addition, AI programs can analyze large amounts of medical data very quickly and may reduce human error caused by fatigue or stress. Of course, serious or complicated illnesses should still be handled by human doctors, but I believe AI could become a valuable tool for improving healthcare access.

students.name: Marcus
students.text: Personally, I do not think AI systems should independently treat patients, even in simple situations. Medicine involves more than identifying symptoms and selecting treatments. Doctors also consider emotional factors, unusual patient histories, and ethical concerns that AI may not fully understand. Furthermore, if an AI system makes a mistake, it may be difficult to determine who is legally responsible. Although AI can assist medical professionals, I believe human doctors must remain in control of all final medical decisions to ensure patient safety and trust.

professor.name: Professor
professor.text: Today, we'll discuss the impact of artificial intelligence on employment. In recent years, AI applications have increasingly been used to perform tasks that were once handled by entry-level white-collar workers, such as data analysis, customer service, and basic accounting. Some economists argue that this trend is a major reason for rising youth unemployment because companies now hire fewer inexperienced workers. Others believe AI is only changing the nature of work and creating new opportunities for young people. What is your opinion? Do you think AI is the main cause of higher youth unemployment? Why or why not?

students.name: Sophia
students.text: I believe AI is largely responsible for higher youth unemployment. In the past, companies hired recent graduates to do repetitive office tasks and gain experience. Now, AI tools can complete many of these assignments faster and at a lower cost. As a result, businesses reduce hiring and expect applicants to already possess advanced skills. This creates a serious problem for young workers because they cannot gain experience if no one gives them an opportunity to start. In my opinion, governments and companies should create training programs to help young people adapt to the changing job market.

students.name: Daniel
students.text: Personally, I do not think AI is the main reason for youth unemployment. Economic conditions, inflation, and weak job markets have existed long before AI became common. In addition, technological change has always replaced some jobs while creating others. For example, AI is generating demand for workers in areas such as cybersecurity, software development, and digital marketing. Instead of blaming AI, schools should focus on teaching practical and technical skills that are more useful in today's economy.
